for the first part the equilibrium occurs when dP/dt equals zero so 0.1 times P times one minus P over one thousand equals ten plus five sine of pi t over six which yields a transcendental equation without a simple constant solution because h(t) varies with time yet if we assume an average harvest of ten then P_e equals approximately four hundred and forty six or five hundred and fifty four turtles
for the second part with K(t) equal to one thousand minus three t the equilibrium condition becomes 0.1 times P times one minus P over one thousand minus three t equals ten plus five sine of pi t over six and the population becomes unsustainable when the carrying capacity drops so low that no positive P satisfies the equation which happens when K(t) falls below the minimum required for survival given the harvest rate so setting K(t) to zero gives t equals three hundred and thirty three point three three years but realistically the population collapses earlier when the maximum possible growth cannot offset the minimum harvest of ten turtles per year so the time when P_e(t) becomes non positive occurs before t reaches three hundred and thirty three years specifically when one thousand minus three t is less than the threshold where r times K times one quarter equals ten which means K must be at least four hundred so one thousand minus three t equals four hundred yields t equals two hundred years after which the population cannot sustain itself and will decline to zero
noon
